title: Run the Tipper Operation Cycle From the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen
summary: Use the primary operator station HMI screen, Visu_MCP_Dual, to place the tipper in Auto mode and start or stop the tipper operation cycle. The source identifies AUTO, CYCLE START, and CYCLE STOP as the normal operating controls and states that green illuminated bars above buttons indicate which controls are active.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during normal operator control of the tipper from the primary operator station HMI when you need to place the tipper in automatic mode and start or stop the tipper operation cycle from the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
not_for: Do not use JOG as part of normal operator operation; the source states JOG is for manually controlling the end effector and gripper and the candidate notes it is not part of normal operator operation.
Not for recovery or troubleshooting when the required control is not active or the tipper does not respond as expected; the supplied source section does not provide further recovery steps.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual primary operator screen to verify active controls, place the tipper in Auto mode, start or stop the cycle, and review informational banner messages.

safety_notes: Use the Visu_MCP_Dual screen as the primary operator screen for normal tipper operation.
Green illuminated bars above buttons indicate which controls are active.
Do not use JOG as part of normal operator operation in this runbook.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Operator access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The operator can access or navigate to the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.
The required control buttons are active as indicated by green illuminated bars above the buttons.
The system must be in Auto mode before CYCLE START or CYCLE STOP are used for cycle control.

visual_references.description: Primary operator HMI screen for the tipper machine control panel showing AUTO, JOG, REF, CYCLE START, CYCLE STOP, and the informational banner.
Operator station display controls showing how to navigate to HMI screens including Visu_MCP_Dual using F3.
Operator station HMI data screen showing the informational banner and message navigation arrows referenced for reviewing alarms or informational messages.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen
steps.purpose: Access the primary operator screen used for normal tipper control.
steps.instruction: Open or navigate to the Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen on the operator station HMI.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MCP_Dual control panel is displayed and available for operator use.
steps.healthy_condition: The primary operator screen is visible and the tipper control buttons can be viewed.
steps.failure_condition: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be reached or the operator cannot view the control panel.

actions.label: Navigate to Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Open or navigate to the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen-navigation controls and the mapping showing F3 to Visu_MCP_Dual.
The Operator Station Control Panel image showing the Visu_MCP_Dual primary screen and main control buttons.

stop_or_escalate_if: You cannot access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen from the operator station HMI.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual" screen) has buttons that control tipper movement and allows for fault reset. This is the primary screen that station operators will need to use.
The listed control mapping includes F3 to "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.

steps.title: Verify active cycle controls
steps.purpose: Confirm the operator is using the correct controls and determine which buttons are currently active.
steps.instruction: Identify the AUTO, CYCLE START, and CYCLE STOP buttons on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen. Verify which controls are active by checking for green illuminated bars above the buttons.
steps.expected_result: The operator can identify the cycle control buttons and determine which ones are active.
steps.healthy_condition: The required buttons are visible and active status is clearly indicated by green illuminated bars above the buttons.
steps.failure_condition: The required control is not active, the active-state indication is unclear, or the needed button cannot be identified.

actions.label: Identify cycle controls
actions.exact_action: Identify the AUTO, CYCLE START, and CYCLE STOP buttons.

actions.label: Check active indicators
actions.exact_action: Verify which controls are active by checking for green illuminated bars above the buttons.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: AUTO, CYCLE START, and CYCLE STOP buttons and the green bars above buttons that indicate active controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: The required control is not active.
The tipper does not respond as expected and no further recovery steps are provided in this source section.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The bars above the buttons illuminate green to indicate which buttons are active.

The AUTO button runs the tipper in automatic mode. The CYCLE START starts the tipper operation cycle when the system is on "Auto" mode. The CYCLE STOP button stops the tipper operation cycle when the system is in "Auto" mode.

steps.title: Place the tipper in Auto mode
steps.purpose: Set the tipper to the operating mode required for cycle control.
steps.instruction: Press AUTO to run the tipper in automatic mode.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is placed in automatic mode.
steps.healthy_condition: The system is in Auto mode and ready for cycle control from the HMI.
steps.failure_condition: AUTO is not active or pressing AUTO does not place the tipper in automatic mode.

actions.label: Press AUTO
actions.exact_action: Press AUTO to run the tipper in automatic mode.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The AUTO button on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Pressing AUTO does not place the tipper in automatic mode.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AUTO button runs the tipper in automatic mode.

steps.title: Start the tipper cycle
steps.purpose: Begin the tipper operation cycle after Auto mode is enabled.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE START to start the tipper operation cycle when the system is in Auto mode.
steps.healthy_condition: The cycle starts while the system is in Auto mode.
steps.failure_condition: CYCLE START is not active or pressing it does not start the tipper operation cycle.

actions.label: Press CYCLE START
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE START to start the tipper operation cycle when the system is in Auto mode.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The CYCLE START button on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system is not in Auto mode.
Pressing CYCLE START does not start the tipper operation cycle.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The CYCLE START starts the tipper operation cycle when the system is on "Auto" mode.

steps.title: Stop the tipper cycle
steps.purpose: Stop the tipper operation cycle from the HMI while in Auto mode.
steps.instruction: Use CYCLE STOP to stop the tipper operation cycle when the system is in Auto mode.
steps.healthy_condition: The cycle stops when CYCLE STOP is used in Auto mode.
steps.failure_condition: CYCLE STOP is not active or pressing it does not stop the tipper operation cycle.

actions.label: Press CYCLE STOP
actions.exact_action: Use CYCLE STOP to stop the tipper operation cycle when the system is in Auto mode.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The CYCLE STOP button on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The system is not in Auto mode.
Pressing CYCLE STOP does not stop the tipper operation cycle.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The CYCLE STOP button stops the tipper operation cycle when the system is in "Auto" mode.

steps.title: Review banner messages if needed
steps.purpose: Check alarms or informational messages shown on the operator HMI.
steps.instruction: If needed, review the informational banner for alarms or informational messages and use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through messages.
steps.expected_result: The operator can view current banner messages and move through available messages.
steps.healthy_condition: Banner messages are visible and the operator can navigate through them using the arrows.
steps.failure_condition: Messages cannot be reviewed or the operator cannot navigate through the banner messages.

actions.label: Review informational banner
actions.exact_action: Review the informational banner for alarms or informational messages.

actions.label: Navigate banner messages
actions.exact_action: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to navigate through messages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The informational banner and the arrows on the right side of the banner.
The informational banner for alarms and informational messages with navigation arrows.

stop_or_escalate_if: Banner messages indicate a condition that prevents normal operation.
The operator cannot review or navigate the displayed messages.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 4-19 includes an informational banner for alarms and messages with navigation arrows on the right side.
This operator station screen includes an informational banner for alarms and informational messages with navigation arrows.

success_criteria: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is accessible from the operator station HMI.
The operator can identify AUTO, CYCLE START, and CYCLE STOP on the primary operator screen.
The tipper is placed in automatic mode using AUTO.
The tipper operation cycle can be started using CYCLE START while in Auto mode.
The tipper operation cycle can be stopped using CYCLE STOP while in Auto mode.
The operator can review informational banner messages and navigate through them if needed.
healthy_conditions: Visu_MCP_Dual is displayed as the primary operator screen.
Green illuminated bars above buttons clearly indicate which controls are active.
AUTO places the tipper in automatic mode.
CYCLE START starts the tipper operation cycle in Auto mode.
CYCLE STOP stops the tipper operation cycle in Auto mode.
Informational banner messages are visible and navigable.
failure_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed.
The required control is not active.
AUTO does not place the tipper in automatic mode.
CYCLE START does not start the tipper operation cycle.
CYCLE STOP does not stop the tipper operation cycle.
The operator cannot review or navigate banner messages.
The tipper does not respond as expected and the source section provides no further recovery steps.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the required control is not active.
Stop and escalate if the tipper does not respond as expected.
Use a different source-backed recovery or maintenance procedure for troubleshooting; this source section does not provide recovery steps.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary operator screen showing AUTO, CYCLE START, CYCLE STOP, green active bars, and the informational banner.
Navigation controls and F3 mapping to Visu_MCP_Dual.
Informational banner and message navigation arrows.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual" screen) has buttons that control tipper movement and allows for fault reset. The bars above the buttons illuminate green to indicate which buttons are active. This is the primary screen that station operators will need to use.

The AUTO button runs the tipper in automatic mode. The CYCLE START starts the tipper operation cycle when the system is on "Auto" mode. The CYCLE STOP button stops the tipper operation cycle when the system is in "Auto" mode.

missing_details: The supplied source section does not provide a time estimate for completing this procedure.
The supplied source section does not specify whether production stop is required.
The supplied source section does not specify whether lockout/tagout is required.
The supplied source section does not provide detailed recovery actions if a control is inactive or the tipper does not respond as expected.
The supplied source section does not define explicit role boundaries beyond identifying station operators as the primary users of this screen.
